FRENCH ARE PLANNING POOP PROTESTS IN PARIS

Many Parisians are planning to leave the city before the impending onslaught of Olympic spectators. The French have had qualms about the Games since last year, with reports of more construction, higher metro prices, and more security measures. The news of authorities removing homeless people and cracking down on sex workers also hasn’t endeared the games to the public. And now, protesters are threatening to soil the plans in an inventive way: by defecating in the Seine. 

The protest, Je Chie Dans La Seine Le 23 Juin (“I poop in the Seine on June 23”), is gaining traction on social media with accounts sharing logistics of the protests and memes. Scrutiny of Perplexity’s practices follows a June 11 report from Forbes that accused the startup of stealing at least one of its articles. WIRED investigations confirmed the practice and found further evidence of scraping abuse and plagiarism by systems linked to Perplexity’s AI-powered search chatbot. Engineers for Condé Nast, WIRED’s parent company, block Perplexity’s crawler across all its websites using a robots.txt file. But WIRED found the company had access to a server using an unpublished IP address—44.221.181.252—which visited Condé Nast properties at least hundreds of times in the past three months, apparently to scrape Condé Nast websites.
